skill.name: journal-club-presenter
skill.description: Generate journal club presentation slides with background context, critical evaluation, and discussion questions for academic paper review sessions.
skill.summary: Generate structured journal club slides from paper details for academic presentations.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 147 potential issues (risk score 100/100), but all findings are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections were triggered by decorative '='*70 string repeat patterns in the Python slide generator and by text descriptions containing algorithm-like words. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections were triggered by markdown code fences in documentation. The 'path traversal' findings at SKILL.md:117 and SKILL.md:120 are security checklist items documenting safeguards, not vulnerabilities. The single file write at scripts/main.py:147 is the legitimate core function of the skill. No malicious intent, no network calls, no data exfiltration, and no command injection were found.

low_findings.title: File write operation to user-specified path
low_findings.description: The script writes generated slide output to a file path provided via the --output CLI argument. The default path is journal_club_outline.txt. While this is the intended function of the skill, users should ensure the output path is within the workspace.

low_findings.title: Static analyzer false positive: path traversal in security checklist
low_findings.description: Lines 117 and 120 of SKILL.md contain security checklist items that document path traversal protections: 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)'. The static analyzer matched the '../' pattern in these protective checklist entries, not in actual path traversal code. These are security documentation, not vulnerabilities.

content.user_title: Generate Journal Club Presentation Slides from Research Papers
content.value_statement: Preparing journal club presentations is time-consuming and requires structured critique of research papers. This skill generates a complete slide outline with background, methods analysis, critical evaluation, and discussion questions so you can focus on understanding the paper instead of formatting slides.
actual_capabilities: Generate a structured 9-slide journal club presentation outline from paper metadata
Create title slides with author, journal, and presenter information
Produce background context slides with scientific problem framing
Generate methods overview and key results summary sections
Include strengths and weaknesses critique slides for critical appraisal
Add discussion questions and take-home message slides
limitations: Requires the user to provide paper details as command-line arguments
Does not read or parse PDF files directly
Produces plain text outlines, not formatted PowerPoint or Google Slides files
Cannot evaluate the scientific quality of the paper automatically

use_cases.title: Lab Meeting Presentations
use_cases.description: A researcher needs to present a recent paper at a weekly lab meeting. The skill generates a structured slide outline from the paper details, saving time on formatting.

use_cases.title: Graduate Student Journal Club Training
use_cases.description: A graduate student preparing for their first journal club presentation uses the skill to learn proper paper critique structure, including background, methods analysis, and discussion questions.

use_cases.title: Literature Review Discussion Sessions
use_cases.description: A research group conducting a systematic literature review uses the skill to standardize how each paper is presented and critiqued across multiple sessions.
use_cases.target_user: Research group leaders and principal investigators

prompt_templates.title: Basic Paper Presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate journal club slides for this paper: Title: [paper title], Authors: [author names], Journal: [journal name], Year: [year]. Create a complete slide outline.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick setup for a standard journal club presentation

prompt_templates.title: Presentation with Custom Settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the journal club presenter with these details: Paper title: [title], Authors: [authors], Journal: [journal], Year: [year], Presenter: [name], Output file: [filename]. Use the scripts/main.py entry point.
prompt_templates.scenario: Using the packaged script with specific paper metadata

prompt_templates.title: Critical Appraisal Focus
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a journal club presentation for [paper title] with emphasis on critical appraisal. Include detailed strengths and weaknesses analysis, methodological critique, and challenging discussion questions for an expert audience.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced critical evaluation for experienced researchers

prompt_templates.title: Batch Literature Review Sessions
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to prepare journal club slides for multiple papers in our literature review. For each paper, use the journal club presenter workflow: confirm the paper details, generate the slide outline with background and critique, and save each to a separate output file. Start with: [paper 1 details].

output_examples.input: Generate journal club slides for a paper on CRISPR gene editing published in Nature 2024
output_examples.output: Generated slide outline with 9 slides: Title slide with paper info, Background on gene editing problem, Research Question and Hypothesis, Methods Overview, Key Results summary, Strengths critique, Weaknesses and Limitations, Discussion Questions (5 prompts), and Take-Home Message with 3 key points.

output_examples.input: Create a critical appraisal presentation for a clinical trial paper with expert audience level
output_examples.output: Slide structure includes: Title slide with trial details, Background on clinical need, Research Question with PICO framework, Methods with study design analysis, Results with effect sizes, Strengths and Weaknesses with methodological critique, and Discussion Questions focused on clinical applicability and next steps.

best_practices: Always confirm paper details with the user before generating the slide outline
Run the script validation command first to verify the Python environment is ready
Review the generated outline and fill in placeholder fields with actual paper content
anti_patterns: Do not fabricate paper details, citations, or research findings to fill template placeholders
Do not use this skill for tasks outside academic paper presentation such as writing original research
Do not skip the input validation step before running the slide generation script

faq.question: What does this skill actually generate?
faq.answer: It generates a structured plain text outline with 9 slides covering title, background, research question, methods, results, critique, discussion questions, and take-home message.

faq.question: Does this skill read PDF files?
faq.answer: No. You must provide the paper details such as title, authors, journal, and year as input arguments. The skill does not parse PDF documents.

faq.question: What output format does it produce?
faq.answer: The script produces a plain text file with a formatted slide outline. You can then transfer this content to PowerPoint, Google Slides, or any presentation tool.

faq.question: Can the skill evaluate the quality of a paper?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides a critique template with placeholders for strengths and weaknesses. You must supply the actual evaluation based on your reading of the paper.

faq.question: What Python version is required?
faq.answer: Python 3.10 or later. The skill uses only the Python standard library with no additional packages needed.

faq.question: How do I run the skill?
faq.answer: Run python scripts/main.py --title 'Paper Title' --authors 'Author Names' --journal 'Journal' --year 2024 from the skill directory. Use --help to see all available options.
